description:
# [BUG] Compiler2 artifacts: recover raw integer callable-entry lanes for Enum.reduce

Goal: Compiler2 should preserve a raw integer callable-entry return lane inside the settled Enum.reduce user reducer callable inventory.

Signal:
- cargo test --lib compiler2_abi_ready_derives_only_the_closed_enum_reduce_callable_entries -- --nocapture passes
- cargo test --lib compiler2_emission_ready_includes_the_required_enum_reduce_callable_entries -- --nocapture passes

Why:
- fixing fz-rh2.8.2.6 unblocked the Enum.reduce semantic path far enough to expose a callable-entry contract mismatch.
- Compiler2 intentionally keeps multiple specialized callable entries for one callable identity when the closed executable frontier needs them; the user reducer already had a RawInt lane, but the tests were reading the first matching entry and treating that as the whole callable inventory.
- close this by asserting the real contract: the user reducer callable inventory includes a RawInt lane, the bridge reducer inventory stays ValueRef, and helper lookups must return all entries for a callable identity instead of an arbitrary first match.

## UNBLOCKS

- fz-rh2.8.2.4 Compiler2 backends: switch entry points and remove compiler2 fallback
title: Compiler2 artifacts: recover raw integer callable-entry lanes for Enum.reduce
